prevalence.trend: Increasing (attributed to rising air pollution and tobacco use)

incidence.source: Global Burden of Disease Study (India estimates)

riskFactors: Genetic factors (alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency, rare in India)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Based on GOLD guidelines: Persistent respiratory symptoms + FEV1/FVC <0.7 post-bronchodilator spirometry; staging by FEV1 % predicted

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Low (<10% in high-risk groups; spirometry not routine)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Spirometry gold standard, but often clinical diagnosis in resource-limited settings (dyspnea, chronic cough, wheeze)
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: GOLD-based: Smoking cessation, bronchodilators (SABA/LABA), ICS for exacerbations, pulmonary rehab; affordable generics available
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High undiagnosed cases in rural areas, poor access to spirometry, biomass exposure in 60% households, air quality index often >300 in cities
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: National Tobacco Control Program, increasing awareness via ICMR initiatives, low-cost inhalers through public health facilities
